topic_name: Async I/O with HttpClient and CompletableFuture

exercises.title: Trace sendAsync chain
exercises.scenario: sendAsync returns CF — chain works like any other CF.
hints: supplyAsync returns 'HTTP 200 OK'
thenApply checks contains('200') → 'success'
thenApply toUpperCase → 'SUCCESS'
thenAccept prints it

exercises.title: sendAsync vs send — method names
exercises.scenario: The non-blocking version adds 'Async' to the method name.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank with the async HTTP send method name.

blanks.hint: Async version of send() — returns CF immediately

hints: sendAsync — the async counterpart to send()
Returns CompletableFuture<HttpResponse<T>> immediately

exercises.title: Async chain with mock HTTP
exercises.scenario: Simulate sendAsync, chain thenApply for parsing.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate an async HTTP response. Chain thenApply to extract status and parse a number from the 'body'. Use exceptionally for fallback.

Expected output:
Async result: 42
Fallback: -1
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// Simulate async HTTP: supply body → thenApply parse → exceptionally fallback
hints: thenApply chains transformation of the body
exceptionally catches any RuntimeException and returns -1

exercises.title: Parallel fetch timing comparison
exercises.scenario: Show that parallel async is faster than sequential blocking.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate 4 tasks (each 150ms) two ways: sequential and parallel async. Print elapsed times.

Expected output:
Sequential: ~600ms
Parallel async: ~150ms
Speedup: ~4x
hints: Sequential: 4 × sleep(150) = ~600ms
Parallel: runAsync for each, allOf to wait = ~150ms
Speedup ≈ seqTime / parTime ≈ 4

exercises.title: Async JSON parse chain
exercises.instructions:
Simulate sendAsync returning a mock response. Chain: check status code → parse JSON → extract field. Use Jackson.

Expected output:
City: Bengaluru
Temp: 28°C
hints: thenApply checks status, returns body string
thenApply parses with MAPPER.readTree(body)
Checked exception in thenApply → wrap as RuntimeException

exercises.title: exceptionally for 404 fallback
exercises.scenario: 404 response handled gracefully in async chain.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate async requests for 3 symbols: 2 succeed, 1 returns 404. Use exceptionally to return 'NOT_FOUND' for 404s. Collect and print all results.

Expected output:
TCS: ₹3890
INFY: NOT_FOUND
RELIANCE: ₹2450
hints: exceptionally on each CF — per-request fallback
allOf waits for all; join inside thenRun is instant
futures list preserves submission order

exercises.title: anyOf — fastest CDN wins
exercises.scenario: anyOf returns first completed future — fastest wins.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate 3 CDN endpoints with different delays (300ms, 100ms, 200ms). Use anyOf to get the fastest response.

Expected output:
Fastest CDN responded: cdn2.example.com (100ms)
Total time: ~100ms
hints: anyOf completes when ANY one future completes
cdn2 at 100ms is the fastest
Cast Object result back to CdnResult

exercises.title: allOf with per-request error handling
exercises.scenario: Individual failures handled; others still collected.
exercises.instructions:
5 async requests — 3 succeed, 2 fail. Each failure returns a default. allOf collects all. Print successful and failed counts.

Expected output:
Results collected: 5
Successful: 3
Failed (used default): 2
hints: exceptionally on each CF provides per-request fallback
allOf waits for ALL (including failed ones that returned defaults)
Count by filtering Result::success

exercises.title: Async stock dashboard — Jackson parse chain
exercises.scenario: Full async pipeline: simulate HTTP, parse JSON, display.
exercises.instructions:
Fetch 5 stock quotes in parallel (simulated). Parse each with Jackson. Print sorted dashboard. Show total time.

Expected output (sorted by price desc):
=== Async Stock Dashboard ===
Fetched in ~Nms

TCS          ₹3,890
RELIANCE     ₹2,450
HDFC         ₹1,679
INFY         ₹1,560
WIPRO          ₹412
hints: All 5 CFs start simultaneously — total time ~50ms not 250ms
MAPPER.readTree inside lambda → wrap JsonProcessingException as RuntimeException
Join inside thenRun is safe — all futures already done

exercises.title: Trace allOf ordering
exercises.scenario: allOf fires thenRun only after ALL futures complete; join order is submission order.
hints: allOf fires thenRun when all 3 are done — after 300ms
join() order matches f1, f2, f3 — submission order, not completion order
Output is in join() call order: slow, fast, medium

exercises.title: Async service fan-out — product page assembly
exercises.scenario: 3 async services in parallel; assemble result when all done.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate a product page that fetches: product details (200ms), ratings (150ms), inventory (100ms) — all in parallel. Assemble and print when all done.

Expected output:
=== Product Page ===
Product: Wireless Mouse (₹799)
Rating: 4.3 ★ (218 reviews)
Inventory: 47 units in stock
Assembled in ~200ms
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// Async product page — 3 parallel service calls
hints: All 3 CFs start simultaneously — total ~200ms (slowest)
join() inside thenRun is instant — already done
Not 200+150+100 = 450ms sequential

exercises.title: Sequential async pipeline with thenCompose
exercises.scenario: Dependent async steps — each waits for previous result.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate a 3-step async pipeline: fetch userId → fetch user → fetch user's orders. Each step depends on the previous result. Use thenCompose.

Expected output:
Step 1: Resolved userId = 42
Step 2: Fetched user = Aisha
Step 3: Found 5 orders for Aisha
Pipeline complete
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// Sequential async: thenCompose chain

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Step 1: Resolved userId = 42
Step 2: Fetched user = Aisha
Step 3: Found 5 orders for Aisha
Pipeline complete

hints: thenCompose for steps where next step returns CF
Each step receives the RESULT of the previous step
Total: 80+100+60=240ms sequential — but no threads blocked between steps

exercises.title: Async price comparison across multiple APIs
exercises.scenario: Fan-out to multiple price sources; take the best price.
exercises.instructions:
Fetch price for 'Laptop' from 3 simulated APIs simultaneously (different delays, slightly different prices). Take the LOWEST price. Show which API won.

Expected output:
Fetching from 3 price APIs in parallel...
Flipkart: ₹45999 (180ms)
Amazon:   ₹46499 (120ms)
Meesho:   ₹44999 (200ms)
Best price: ₹44999 from Meesho
Fetched in ~200ms
hints: All 3 start simultaneously — total ~200ms (slowest API)
min() by price to find best deal
Print order may vary (depends on completion time)

exercises.title: Async retry with exponential backoff
exercises.scenario: Retry failed async requests with increasing wait times.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `retryAsync(Supplier<CF<T>>, int maxRetries)` that retries on RuntimeException with 100ms, 200ms, 400ms backoff. Test with a supplier that fails twice then succeeds.

Expected output:
Attempt 1 failed — retrying in 100ms
Attempt 2 failed — retrying in 200ms
Attempt 3 succeeded: result
Final: result
hints: exceptionallyCompose: like exceptionally but returns CF (enables async retry)
Exponential backoff: 100ms, 200ms, 400ms
Recursive call: retryAsync(taskSupplier, maxRetries - 1)

exercises.title: Complete async payment aggregation — Module 11 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full async I/O pipeline: parallel HTTP → JSON parse → validate → aggregate → write report.
exercises.instructions:
Module 11 capstone:
1. Fetch payment records from 3 'APIs' in parallel (simulated, each returns JSON array)
2. Parse each JSON response with Jackson
3. Combine all records
4. Compute: total amount, count by status, failed payments
5. Write JSON summary report to file with Files.writeString
6. Print results

Expected output:
=== Payment Aggregation Report ===
APIs queried: 3
Total payments: 9
Successful: 6  |  Failed: 3
Total amount: ₹[sum]
Report written: report.json

test_cases.expected_stdout:
=== Payment Aggregation Report ===
APIs queried: 3
Total payments: 9
Successful: 6  |  Failed: 3
Total amount: ₹14199
Report written: report.json

hints: TypeReference<List<Payment>>{} for JSON array → List parse
flatMap(List::stream) to merge 3 lists into one
Files.writeString inside thenRun → wrap IOException as RuntimeException
